context: Ralph is a Go 1.24 CLI with an optional Bubble Tea/Lip Gloss TUI in internal/tui and a Vite + React + TypeScript frontend in web/src. Shared backend behavior lives in internal/workflow and internal/shared, including config, runner integrations, runstate, PRD storage, workdir helpers, the shared session facade in internal/shared/session, and the shared translation helpers for checkpoints, event status, and phase mapping. The web side uses internal/web for HTTP handlers, run controllers, SSE, and registry state. The current pattern is to keep presentation logic in the UI layer while workflow state, PRD validation/storage, runner orchestration, and shared read snapshots stay in shared Go packages. Public APIs and routes may change if that helps consolidate logic into shared packages. Tests are table-driven Go *_test.go files for backend code, Vitest for web client code via cd web && npm test, and Playwright e2e tests via cd e2e && npx playwright test. The full feature suite currently runs with go test ./... && cd web && npm test && cd ../e2e && npx playwright test. When web UI files change, embedded assets are regenerated with go generate ./internal/web/...
test_spec: Verify a run can build the same minimal read-only shared snapshot before PRD load, with prompt and phase falling back from driver/checkpoint state; verify a run started from either UI can read the same shared backend snapshot for phase, current story, slice progress, and completed counts; verify approve, revise, follow-up, and implementation-review actions continue to route through one shared session path and still surface the same errors and lifecycle updates; verify resume flows from PRD review, implementation review, follow-up, and completed checkpoints resolve to the same phase in both the TUI and web controller, including the impl_review mismatch; verify shared workflow events keep web registry state and TUI state in sync for running, failing, and completed runs by extending the existing helpers; verify the web API, TUI, and headless runner continue to render identical story progress from the same PRD data after the backend consolidation, even if supporting routes or public API shapes are adjusted to reduce duplication.

stories.title: Adding a shared run snapshot
stories.description: Introduce a UI-agnostic, read-only run snapshot in internal/shared/session and/or internal/workflow that can be built before PRD load and that derives prompt, current PRD, phase, current story, next pending slice, completed story count, total story count, and checkpoint/review state from workflow.Driver, run metadata, and internal/shared/prd helpers. Keep the first snapshot shape minimal and make internal/tui, internal/web, and internal/headless consume it instead of rebuilding the same state locally.

slices.behavior: A shared snapshot can be built for a run before any PRD has been loaded.
slices.red_hint: add a failing test that shared snapshot creation works before PRD load, uses the driver's prompt, and falls back to the checkpoint/registry phase
slices.refactor_hint: Start with a minimal read-only snapshot shape and derive prompt and phase from shared driver state.

slices.behavior: A shared snapshot reports the active story and next pending slice during implementation.
slices.red_hint: add a failing test that the shared snapshot exposes the current story and next pending slice for an in-progress PRD
slices.refactor_hint: Use internal/shared/prd helpers for progress and next-pending selection instead of duplicating that logic in UI code.

slices.behavior: A shared snapshot reports completed and total story counts consistently for mixed PRDs.
slices.red_hint: add a failing test that the shared snapshot reports completed and total story counts from a PRD with both finished and unfinished stories

stories.title: Rewiring UIs to the shared session
stories.description: Update internal/tui, internal/web, and internal/headless/runner.go to consume the shared snapshot and the shared action methods from internal/shared/session. Remove the duplicated PRD read-path reconstruction in internal/web/handlers/runs.go and internal/tui/view.go so the presentation layers only render shared data; keep approve, revise, follow-up, and implementation-review dispatching through session.Session. Public API routes may change if needed to make the shared path the canonical source of truth.

slices.behavior: The TUI resume and implementation screens render from the shared backend snapshot.
slices.red_hint: add a failing test that TUI resume rendering uses shared snapshot data instead of local PRD or checkpoint helpers
slices.refactor_hint: Keep the Bubble Tea model focused on layout and input handling after the shared snapshot lands.

slices.behavior: The web run detail API returns the same progress and story fields derived by the shared backend.
slices.red_hint: add a failing test that GET /api/runs/{id} reflects the shared snapshot values for progress and story slices

slices.behavior: The headless runner reads the same shared snapshot instead of rebuilding progress locally.
slices.red_hint: add a failing test that internal/headless/runner.go consumes the shared snapshot for run state instead of re-deriving it

stories.title: Centralizing resume and event translation
stories.description: Extend the existing workflow.EventStatusPhase and runstate.CheckpointPhase helpers so the TUI and web controller share one source of truth for resuming runs and applying terminal-state updates. Replace the local resumePhase switch in internal/tui/operations.go and the status/phase branching in internal/web/runner/controller.go with those helpers, and fix the impl_review resume mismatch so both entry points agree on the returned phase.

slices.behavior: Every supported checkpoint resolves to the same resume phase in both entry points.
slices.red_hint: add a failing test for shared checkpoint-to-phase resolution across PRD review, implementation review, follow-up, completed, and impl_review checkpoints

slices.behavior: Workflow events map to the same run status and phase updates for TUI and web callers.
slices.red_hint: add a failing test that shared event translation updates running, failed, and completed state consistently

slices.behavior: Terminal events still invoke the web controller completion callback once.
slices.red_hint: add a failing test that terminal event handling still triggers the onTerminal callback exactly once after shared translation
